summary: Dan Shapiro shares a brief cautionary tale about table saw safety after reading about a fatal accident where a splinter kicked back and killed a woodworker. He advocates for wearing a heavy leather shop apron as a potentially life-saving piece of safety equipment. He notes the practical bonus of always having a tape measure handy. The post ends with him convincing a friend to go apron shopping after telling the story to the friend's wife.
key_points: A full-length leather shop apron could prevent fatal injuries from table saw kickback
Storytelling about safety risks is an effective way to motivate others to take precautions
Practical shop safety gear can serve double duty — protection plus convenient tool storage
money_quotes: Nothing's for sure, but a heavy leather shop apron might have saved him.
Plus, you've always got your tape measure handy–beats the hell out of my current system of strategically placing tape measures every five feet around the shop.
Get a heavy apron–you'll thank me for it.
key_insight: Simple, inexpensive safety gear like a leather shop apron can be the difference between a close call and a fatal workshop accident.
